sections.text: Every page on the internet has its own address, just like every house has its own address. That address is called a URL, and learning to read one helps you understand exactly where you are going on the web.

sections.title: What is a URL?
sections.text: URL stands for Uniform Resource Locator. It is the full address you type or click to visit a specific page on the internet. A URL tells your browser which protocol to use, which website to visit, and sometimes which exact page or file on that website.

sections.title: What is a Domain Name?
sections.text: A domain name is the main part of a URL, the easy to remember name like example.com. It is just one piece of the full URL. Think of the domain name as the name of a building, while the full URL also tells you which room inside that building you want to visit.

sections.title: Breaking Down a URL
items: Protocol: the part like https:// that tells the browser how to talk to the server.
Domain name: the part like example.com that tells the browser which website to visit.
Path: the part after the domain, like /about, that points to a specific page on that website.
Query parameters: extra information after a question mark, like ?search=shoes.
Putting it all together, a full URL might look like https://example.com/about?search=shoes.

sections.title: Think of it like a Home Address
sections.text: A domain name is like the name of an apartment building, such as Sunshine Apartments. A full URL is like the complete address, including the building name, the floor number, and the apartment number. It tells you exactly where to go, not just which building.
Domain names have to be registered and paid for every year, which is why companies sometimes lose their domain name if they forget to renew it.

terms.definition: Uniform Resource Locator, the full address used to find a specific page on the internet.
The easy to remember main part of a URL, like example.com.
The part of the URL, like https, that says how the browser should talk to the server.
The part of the URL after the domain name that points to a specific page.
Extra pieces of information added to a URL after a question mark.

options: The full address used to find a page on the internet
questions.explanation: A URL is the complete address you use to find a specific page on the internet, including the protocol, domain name, and sometimes a path.

options: The easy to remember main part of a URL, like example.com
The text shown on a webpage
questions.explanation: A domain name is the easy to remember part of a URL, like example.com, that identifies a particular website.

questions.text: In the URL https://example.com/about, what is /about called?
questions.explanation: The /about part is called the path, and it points to a specific page within the website.
